label: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)

node.label: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)
node.definition: A policy is adjusted toward preferred behavior while controlling deviation from a reference model.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is to connect Preference learning, Reward modeling to downstream capabilities including Direct and online preference optimization, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
examples: substrate for Direct and online preference optimization
substrate for Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
substrate for Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Reward modeling.

dependencies.rationale: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Reward modeling.
Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.

sentence_units.text: A policy is adjusted toward preferred behavior while controlling deviation from a reference model.

The role of Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is to connect Preference learning, Reward modeling to downstream capabilities including Direct and online preference optimization, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
substrate for Direct and online preference optimization
substrate for Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
substrate for Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)

A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Reward modeling.
Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Reward modeling.
Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
